{{Quote|There is a grave disturbance in the ''Force''! We have a new enemy who could bring about our ''destruction''--the ''son'' of ''Skywalker''!|[[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]|<ref name="Star Wars (1977) 42">[[Star Wars (1977) 42|''Star Wars'' (1977) 42]]</ref>}}
'''''Star Wars 42: The Empire Strikes Back: To Be a Jedi''''' is the forty-second issue in the [[Star Wars (1977)|''Marvel Star Wars'']] series of comics.

This issue includes a cover gallery displaying the British variants of Star Wars #[[Star Wars (1977) 39|39]]–[[Star Wars (1977) 44|44]].

==Plot summary==
[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] watches as his recently landed [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing fighter]] begins to slowly sink into the swamps of [[Dagobah/Legends|Dagobah]]. He endeavors to set up camp until he meets a diminutive green gnome who unceremoniously begins rifling through his belongings. The gnome learns that Luke is looking for a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] named [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] and volunteers to take him to him. They return to the gnome's dwelling and Luke begins to act impatient. It is revealed that the gnome is in fact, master Yoda. Yoda feels that Luke is too old and reckless to learn the ways of the Jedi, but the voice of [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] encourages Yoda to train him. Yoda begins putting Luke through an extensive training period of physical and mental exercises. He pushes him to expand his knowledge and use of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]].

While Luke is training on Dagobah, [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]], and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] continue to outmaneuver Imperial gun ships through an asteroid field. Han angles the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] down towards one of the larger asteroids and swoops into a cave inside one of the craters. He lands the Falcon deep within the recesses of the cave as Imperial [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE bombers]] drop their payloads onto the outer surface of the asteroid. But they only touch down for a brief moment, until they realize that the "cave" they just entered is in fact a giant [[Exogorth/Legends|space slug]]. Han gooses the Falcon and they zip out of the mouth of the beast before it can consume them.

Re-entering the battle with the TIE fighters, Han turns the ''Millennium Falcon'' around and executes a suicide-run straight for the [[Avenger/Legends|''Avenger'']]. He veers off at the last second and then attaches the ship to the rear of the star destroyer's [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]]. Captain [[Lorth Needa/Legends|Needa]] of the ''Avenger'' is now forced to tell [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] that he lost the ''Falcon''.

Meanwhile, Darth Vader has a private communiqué with [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. Palpatine speaks of a new enemy – Luke Skywalker. Vader suggests that rather than kill Skywalker, they should instead attempt to turn him. Palpatine agrees with the plan and tells Vader to make preparations to capture Skywalker alive.

Han and the others wait for the Imperial fleet to make their regularly scheduled garbage dump before entering [[Lightspeed/Legends|lightspeed]]. As they do so, Han detaches the ''Falcon'' from the side of the ''Avenger'' and floats unobtrusively away with the rest of the garbage. Vader hires a group of [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] to track down and capture the ''Millennium Falcon'', and one of these hunters, [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], begins following Solo in his own ship, [[Slave I/Legends|''Slave I'']].

With the Empire safely behind him, Han makes a course heading toward the planet, [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]]. Bespin is the last known hangout of an old friend of his – [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]].

==Continuity==
This is Chapter Four of the six part adaptation of [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']].

Behind the scenes appearances of [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi's]] [[Force ghost/Legends|Force ghost]] and [[Zuckuss/Legends|Zuckuss]]. A [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] appears partially obscured on the splash page who is presumably intended to be Zuckuss, though he looks nothing at all like the character that most people are familiar with.

1st comic book appearances of [[4-LOM/Legends|4-LOM]], [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], [[Bossk/Legends|Bossk]], [[Dengar/Legends|Dengar]] and [[IG-88B/Legends|IG-88B]]. [[Bossk/Legends|Bossk's]] flight suit is incorrectly colored purple throughout this issue. It should be yellow.

1st full comic book appearance of [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]]. He made a cameo introduction in Star Wars #41. The artwork used to illustrate Yoda in this issue is different then that used in ''[[The Marvel Comics Illustrated Version of Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back]]''. Yoda is seen significantly smaller than his movie or regular appearances.

1st comic book appearance of [[Lorth Needa/Legends|Lorth Needa]]. Dies in this issue.

[[Lucasfilm]] forbade [[Marvel Comics]] from actually illustrating the space slug for the official movie comic adaptation. As the comic book adaptations were being released concurrently with the release of [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']], the staff of Lucasfilm wanted the appearance of the space slug to be a surprise.
